title: Improving Structure-Based Virtual Screening of Kinases using AlphaFold2 with Multi-State Modeling
author: Jinung Song
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Kinases are one of the major drug targets in drug discovery. However, it is observed that both experimentally determined structures and AlphaFold2 predicted structures are biased toward the active state conformation. The bias influences the chance to discover the diverse kinase inhibitors via structure-based virtual screening. To overcome the issue, we propose a protocol to model diverse kinase structures by applying multi-state modeling to AlphaFold2. With the predicted conformations, we benchmarked the ensemble virtual screening performance. The ensemble docking using our kinase modeling protocol outperformed the standard AlphaFold2 models, showing particularly enhanced performance for ligand sets with diverse chemical characteristics. We expect this method will facilitate the discovery of novel kinase inhibitors with a wider range of chemical scaffolds, thereby broadening the possibilities for more target-specific and effective therapeutics.
